zh.lifeSummary: 當Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich於1197年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾維爾斯布恩 海斯廷斯時，他的父親Herbert de Walton30歲，母親Mrs. Margaret De Walton of Hereford30歲。他於1229年與Agnes在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他於1258年被列為bishop of norwich。他去世於1266年1月在英國英格蘭諾福爾可諾維其享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich于1197年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾維爾斯布恩 海斯廷斯时，他的父亲Herbert de Walton30岁，母亲Mrs. Margaret De Walton of Hereford30岁。他于1229年与Agnes在英国英格兰諾福爾可諾維其结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他于1258年被列为bishop of norwich。他去世于1266年1月在英国英格兰諾福爾可諾維其享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Walton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Walton, which are named with Old English walh ‘foreigner, Briton, serf’ (genitive plural wala), wald ‘woodland’, wall ‘wall’, or wælla ‘well, spring, stream’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

History: George Walton (1741–1804) signed the Declaration of Independence. He was born in Prince Edward County, VA, whither his grandfather had emigrated from England in 1682. He moved to Savannah, GA, and became governor of GA and a prominent jurist.

nameMeanings.name: Bishop
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English bissop, biscop, Old English bisc(e)op ‘bishop’, which comes via Latin from Greek episkopos ‘overseer’. The Greek word was adopted early in the Christian era as a title for an overseer of a local community of Christians, and has yielded cognates in every European language: French évêque, Italian vescovo, Spanish obispo, Russian yepiskop, German Bischof, etc. The word came to be applied as a surname for a variety of reasons, among them a supposed resemblance in bearing or appearance to a bishop, and selection as the ‘boy bishop’ on Saint Nicholas's Feast Day. In some instances the surname is from the rare Middle English (Old English) personal name Biscop ‘bishop’. As an Irish surname it is adopted for Mac Giolla Easpaig, meaning ‘servant of the bishop’ (see Gillespie ). In North America, this surname has absorbed, by assimilation and translation, at least some of continental European cognates, e.g. German Bischoff , Polish, Rusyn, Czech, and Slovak Biskup , Slovenian Škof (see Skoff ).

variants.name: Bischoff
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Bernhard, Hans, Klaus, Lorenz, Alois, Armin, Arno, Dieter, Fritz.

German: from Middle High German bischof, Middle Low German bischop ‘bishop’, probably applied as a nickname for someone with a pompous manner or as a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a bishop. See also Bishop .

variants.name: Bispo
meanings: Portuguese and Galician: nickname from bispo ‘bishop’ (see Bishop ).

variants.name: Gillespie
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Donegal): shortened Anglicized form of Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Easbuig, borrowed into Irish as Mac Giolla Easpaig, a patronymic from a personal name meaning ‘servant of the bishop’.

variants.name: Episcopo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Orazio, Pasquale, Rocco.

Italian: from episcopo ‘bishop’ (from Latin episcopus (see Bishop ), probably a nickname for someone who had a pious manner or perhaps a metonymic occupational name for someone in the service of a bishop).

variants.name: Dean
meanings: English: topographic name from Middle English dene ‘valley’ (Old English denu), or a habitational name from any of several places in various parts of England named Dean or Dene from this word.

English: nickname or occupational name for the servant of a dean or nickname for someone thought to resemble a dean. A dean was an ecclesiastical official, the head of a chapter of canons or a church official with jurisdiction over a sub-division of an archdeaconry. Though no doubt some deans had illegitimate children, they were officially celibate, and in the main the surname is probably a nickname in origin, similar to Bishop , Prior , Priest , and Monk . The Middle English word deen, dien, dein, is a borrowing of Old French d(e)ien, doien from Latin decanus (originally a leader of ten men, from decem ‘ten’), and thus is a cognate of Deacon .
English: from the Middle English personal name Deyne (or Dene) a rhyming pet form of Reynald (see Reginald ).

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Norwich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Leicestershire): habitational name from the city of Norwich in Norfolk. The placename derives from Old English north ‘north, northern’ + wīc ‘specialized or outlying farm’.

variants.name: Northridge
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Norridge in Upton Scudamore (Wiltshire) or from a similarly named but unidentified places elsewhere. The placename derives from Middle English north ‘north’ + rigge ‘ridge’ (Old English north, hrycg). There may have been some confusion with Norwich .

nameMeanings.name: Simon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

variants.name: Simen
meanings: Usual English form of Simeon , borne in the New Testament by various characters: two apostles, a brother of Jesus, a Pharisee, a leper, a tanner, a sorcerer (who offered money for the gifts of the Holy Ghost, giving rise to the term simony), and the man who carried Jesus's cross to the Crucifixion.

en.lifeSummary: When Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich was born in 1197, in Wellesbourne Hastings, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Herbert de Walton, was 30 and his mother, Mrs. Margaret De Walton of Hereford, was 30. He married Agnes in 1229, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. In 1258, his occupation is listed as bishop of norwich. He died in January 1266, in Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69.

Il a épousé Agnes en 1229, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. En 1258, sa profession de son indiquée est bishop of norwich. Il est décédé en janvier 1266, à Norwich, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwichが1197年にイギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアウエレスボウルン ハスティングズで生まれた時，父Herbert de Waltonは30歳，母Mrs. Margaret De Walton of Herefordは30歳であった。1229年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチでAgnesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。1258年，職業はbishop of norwichと記録されている。彼は1266年1月にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクノーウィチで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich(이)가 1197년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 웰스번 하스틴그스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Herbert de Walton(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Margaret De Walton of Hereford(은)는 30세였다. 그는 1229년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에서 Agnes(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 1258년, 그의 직업은 bishop of norwich이었다. 그는 1266년 1월 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 노위치에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich nasceu em 1197, em Wellesbourne Hastings, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Herbert de Walton, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Margaret De Walton of Hereford, tinha 30 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Simon de Walton - Bishop of Norwich родился в 1197 г. в местности веллесбурн хастингс, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Herbert de Walton было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Margaret De Walton of Hereford, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Agnes в 1229 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. В 1258 году он работал по профессии bishop of norwich. Он умер январь 1266 г. в местности норвич, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет.

th.Norwich, Norfolk, England, United Kingdo: เนอรวิช, เนอรโฟลก, อังกฤษ, สหราชอาณาจักร

to.between 8 January 1189 and 7 January 119: mei he 8 Sānuali 1189 ki he 7 Sānuali 1190
